serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of baked chicken thigh is approximately 100 g (one medium thigh), providing around 227 kcal, 23 g protein, and 15 g fat.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, thigh, meat and skin, cooked, roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 05178)
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA), Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are taken from the Singapore HPB Foodo database entry for baked chicken thigh (cr_id F11010006, confidence 0.95), which is an approved primary regional source. Saturated fat (4.2 g), monounsaturated fat (6.0 g), polyunsaturated fat (3.2 g), trans fat (0.1 g), cholesterol (93 mg), and potassium (250 mg) are supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB No. 05178 (chicken thigh, meat and skin, cooked, roasted), which is an approved secondary global source. HPB Foodo is the primary source; USDA SR Legacy is the secondary source for fields not reported by HPB.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed per HPB Foodo (F11010006) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB No. 05178
gi_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore; U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Baked chicken thigh contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g as confirmed by both HPB Foodo (cr_id F11010006) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB No. 05178. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI value is assigned. Per Project Veritas v2 rules for low/zero-carbohydrate foods, glycemic_index is set to null and gi_category is set to not_applicable.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (73 mg/100 g) and absence of added salt in plain preparation
hypertension.guidance: Plain baked chicken thigh is low in sodium (73 mg/100 g) and is suitable for hypertension management; avoid high-sodium marinades, sauces, or seasoning blends that can substantially increase sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high protein supports satiety and glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Baked chicken thigh contains no carbohydrate or sugar and will not raise blood glucose; its high protein content (23 g/100 g) supports satiety and lean muscle maintenance, making it a suitable protein source for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat (15 g/100 g) with saturated fat approximately 4.2 g/100 g; skin-on preparation increases saturated fat load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Baked chicken thigh provides moderate fat with approximately 4.2 g saturated fat per 100 g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should remove the skin before eating to reduce saturated fat intake, and avoid high-fat sauces or glazes.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of anti-inflammatory nutrients (omega-3, polyphenols, antioxidants, fibre); saturated fat below 5 g/100 g gate but no positive anti-inflammatory factor present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Baked chicken thigh is not classified as RA-suitable because it provides no positive anti-inflammatory factors; pair with omega-3-rich foods, anti-inflammatory herbs (turmeric, ginger), or fibre-rich vegetables to improve the meal's RA nutritional profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Baked chicken thigh is a cooked poultry product prepared by oven-baking a bone-in or boneless chicken thigh, typically at 180–220°C until the internal temperature reaches 74°C. The thigh is the dark-meat cut from the upper leg of the chicken, known for its richer flavour and higher fat content compared to the breast. When baked, the exterior develops a golden-brown, slightly crisp skin while the interior remains moist and tender. The flavour is savoury and mildly fatty, with a juicy texture that holds up well to dry-heat cooking. Nutritionally, baked chicken thigh provides 227 kcal, 23 g protein, 15 g fat (approximately 4.2 g saturated), and 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g, making it a high-protein, zero-carbohydrate food suitable for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and high-protein dietary patterns. Sodium is low at 73 mg/100 g in plain preparation. Calcium is 22 mg and iron is 0.92 mg per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable due to negligible carbohydrate content. Clinically, it is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes and hypertension management in plain preparation; individuals with cardiovascular disease should remove the skin to reduce saturated fat intake. It is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Common serving size is approximately 100 g (one medium thigh).

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11010006, confidence 0.95), which is an approved primary regional source. Saturated fat, fatty acid fractions, cholesterol, and potassium supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB No. 05178, which is an approved secondary global source. All values are internally consistent and plausible for baked chicken thigh.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source (F11010006); baked chicken thigh is a pure protein-and-fat food with no carbohydrate by composition — confirmed zero. Sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source — confirmed zero. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source — confirmed zero. Saturated_fat_g (4.2 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (6.0 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (3.2 g), trans_fat_g (0.1 g), cholesterol_mg (93 mg), and potassium_mg (250 mg) are supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB No. 05178 (chicken thigh, meat and skin, cooked, roasted) as these fields are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient data for these fields was not imputed to avoid over-imputation beyond the two approved sources used; these should be completed in a future enrichment pass using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB No. 05178. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g carbohydrate per 100 g) — confirmed per HPB Foodo (F11010006) and USDA SR Legacy NDB No. 05178. This record is a generic food record; no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese cuisine classification applies, so no mandatory local-language names are required under G6.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Baked Chicken Thigh (F11010006) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8. G2 compliance achieved by citing USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB No. 05178 as the named approved secondary source in nutrient_source, with HPB Foodo as the primary source. Q4 GI completeness achieved by setting glycemic_index to JSON null, gi_category to not_applicable, and gi_source to reference the approved sources that confirm zero carbohydrate as the basis for the not-applicable determination. Core macros complete and zero carbohydrate confirmed and justified. All -1 micronutrient fields documented in notes. Health context covers Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance. Aliases consistent between flat array and structured object. Ontology anchors are defensible and honest.
